title: Legal Battles and Agentic AI Growth
body_md:
**TL;DR**

- Apple has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI alleging theft of trade secrets.
- Interest in agentic AI is rising as users experiment with personal automation.
- Meta discontinued its Muse Image feature on Instagram following privacy backlash.
- OpenAI launched GPT-5.6 and the enterprise-focused ChatGPT Work platform.

The AI landscape is currently defined by a significant legal conflict between Apple and OpenAI. Apple has initiated a lawsuit against the company, alleging that OpenAI stole trade secrets and poached employees to develop its own hardware. This development marks a sharp decline in the partnership between the two tech giants, which previously focused on integrating AI services into Apple devices.

In the consumer and enterprise space, agentic AI is seeing a surge in interest as developers and users explore autonomous task management. OpenAI has leaned into this trend with the release of its GPT-5.6 model family and the launch of ChatGPT Work, an enterprise platform designed for workplace automation. Meanwhile, Meta has faced a setback, pulling its Muse Image feature from Instagram after users and agencies raised concerns regarding privacy and the unauthorized use of public content for image generation.
